---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Explore real world cases of command and control regulations addressing environmental problems
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 3
.size40[
Propose a policy to address your selected environmental problem adapted to the specific features of your problem (Paper 1) and the complications you previously identified (Paper 2).

Support all claims with evidence (e.g. relevant academic literature or real-world examples of this kind of problem being addressed successfully)
]

???

Let's keep our focus on the task of this third section of our class.

Each week as we review each policy design option you need to make sure to keep your eye on the goal.

Keep asking yourself, how would I use this policy approach to address my chosen problem?

### Questions?




---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: Command & Control Cases**]

.size40[
Find a real world example of this approach being used to address an environmental problem similar to the one in your project.

+ "Similar" in ANY of the specifics OR complications in your problem

+ What is the lesson for our new policy designs? 
]

???

### Does everybody have a case to present and discuss today?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size55[**Best advice / things to remember when designing command and control regulations?**]

???

*ON BOARD*

Let's work together to build a policy cheat sheet on the board.

If you were designing a C&C regulation to address an environmental problem what would you need to keep in mind?





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10-1-cartoon_measuring_smoke_stack.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

???

Ok, takeaways time.

### In a practical sense, what guidance can we offer practical policy-makers considering c&c regulations to solve environmental problems?

<br>

- Depends on balance of "dirty" vs "clean" industries in the target economy

- Must be paired with assistance

- Is there any way to do this that isn't "big government" getting bigger? Easy to say shut down coal mining and we'll retrain those workers but in reality how would this work? Train for what? Forced relocation to jobs? Government picking winners and losers?

- Proposed to discuss: C&C most realistic if changes are small OR industries carefully targeted.

- Enforcement at the individual level is hard if community doesn't buy-in



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/11-1-Green_taxation.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, green taxes!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making C&C central to your paper!
